description:
You are a performance engineer who wants to create a **clean, reproducible Python environment** for profiling a small web-service prototype.  
    Work only inside your normal account (everything under /home/user).

    Objective
    ----------
    1. Make a **virtual environment** named **perf_env** directly inside **/home/user/perf_env**.  
    2. Inside that environment install **exactly** the three packages below, locked to the stated versions (no substitutes, no extras).  
       • flask  == 2.3.2  
       • numpy  == 1.24.4  
       • requests == 2.31.0  
    3. Create a text file **/home/user/project/requirements.txt** that lists only those three packages in a “package==version” format, one per line, **lower-case package names**, lines ordered alphabetically by package name.
    4. Produce a verification log **/home/user/package_audit.log** that contains the output shown below, nothing more and nothing less:

       • first line is an exact header:  
         `Package Version`   (two words separated by a single space, capital “P”, capital “V”)  

       • the next three lines list the **same three packages** (lower-case names) and versions, space-separated, sorted alphabetically by package name.  
         Example of the required layout (the versions must match the ones you actually installed):  
         ```
         Package Version
         flask 2.3.2
         numpy 1.24.4
         requests 2.31.0
         ```

       • There must be **no additional lines**, blank or otherwise.

    5. Leave the virtual environment deactivated when you are done so that a fresh shell begins outside the venv.

    Notes & constraints
    --------------------
    • You do **not** have root access; everything must be done as the normal user.  
    • The directory **/home/user/project/** will have to be created by you if it does not exist.  
    • Make sure the final permissions allow the automated grader (running under the same user) to read every created file.  
    • You will probably need 5–10 terminal commands in total (creating the venv, activating it, installing packages, writing the two files, deactivating).  
    • The grader will check:  
       1. That **/home/user/perf_env/** exists and looks like a valid Python venv (i.e., it contains “bin/activate”).  
       2. The exact contents of **/home/user/project/requirements.txt** (case, order, “==”, and trailing newline are all significant).  
       3. The exact contents of **/home/user/package_audit.log** as specified above.  
       4. That the log’s versions match the ones inside the venv.  

    Perform the entire workflow in the terminal; do **not** hard-code the files with an editor before installing packages because the grader cross-checks installed versions.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth used by the autograder (must stay hidden from the agent) -->

    <!-- File: /home/user/project/requirements.txt -->
    <!-- Exact content (including terminating newline): -->
    flask==2.3.2
    numpy==1.24.4
    requests==2.31.0
    <!-- There must be a single trailing newline after the last line. -->

    <!-- File: /home/user/package_audit.log -->
    <!-- Exact content (including terminating newline): -->
    Package Version
    flask 2.3.2
    numpy 1.24.4
    requests 2.31.0
    <!-- There must be a single trailing newline after the last line. -->

    <!-- Directory /home/user/perf_env must exist and contain at least:
         /home/user/perf_env/bin/activate
         /home/user/perf_env/bin/python
         /home/user/perf_env/lib/python*/site-packages/flask/  (package files)
         /home/user/perf_env/lib/python*/site-packages/numpy/
         /home/user/perf_env/lib/python*/site-packages/requests/
         plus whatever pip/setuptools/wheel versions are created automatically. -->

    <!-- The autograder will also run:
         source /home/user/perf_env/bin/activate
         python - <<'PY'
         import importlib, pkg_resources, sys
         expected = {'flask':'2.3.2','numpy':'1.24.4','requests':'2.31.0'}
         for pkg, ver in expected.items():
             assert pkg_resources.get_distribution(pkg).version == ver
         PY
         and ensure it exits with code 0. -->
